text: Eve here with the Q1 product priorities: 1) Atlas transaction ledger to production with Postgres, 2) Horizon beta launch with TypeScript/React dashboard, 3) Phoenix Kafka migration complete, 4) Orion ML alpha for internal testing.

The core value proposition for enterprise customers: Atlas gives them ACID-compliant transactions backed by Postgres. No more MongoDB inconsistency issues. This is the differentiator Nathan is leading with in sales conversations.

Feature request from customers: real-time notifications when Atlas transactions complete. Bob's Phoenix Kafka stream is perfect for this — we should build the Horizon notification layer on top of the Kafka consumer. Carol, can you scope this?

The Horizon dashboard v1 scope is locked. Carol confirmed: React + TypeScript, GraphQL API, real-time Kafka event subscription, transaction history from Atlas Postgres. No Orion ML features in v1 — that's v1.1. Eve approved the scope.

Morgan is leading the Prism analytics feature — real-time aggregates on top of Atlas transactions and Phoenix events. The key decision: do we build Prism on Kafka Streams (Bob's recommendation) or batch Postgres queries (Alice's recommendation)?

Decided: Prism will use Kafka Streams for real-time aggregates with results stored in Postgres. This was agreed by Alice, Bob, and Morgan after the architecture review. Carol will build the Horizon views for Prism data.

The v2 launch is TBD. Key blockers being tracked in Jira: 1) Atlas scale to 50k TPS (Alice), 2) Orion prediction accuracy above 95% (Dave), 3) Horizon v2 redesign (Carol + Skyler). Nathan needs a v2 date for the enterprise pipeline.

Blake has done the customer research for Atlas v2 features. Top asks: bulk transaction import, webhook notifications via Phoenix, advanced filtering in Horizon. Alice and Bob will scope the technical work in Jira next sprint.

The pricing model decision is pending. Open question: should Orion ML inference calls be metered separately? We need to decide before the enterprise sales push. Eve, Nathan, and Alice are working through the unit economics this week.

Orion alpha feedback from internal users: the PyTorch-powered predictions are accurate but the Horizon integration needs work. Dave is fixing the FastAPI endpoint latency. Carol is improving the prediction UI in the Horizon dashboard.

The Meridian developer API is a core product investment for Q2. Carol will lead the GraphQL API design with input from Alice on the Atlas data model. Avery is writing the API documentation. Nathan needs this for self-serve enterprise customers.

User feedback from Horizon beta: customers love the Kafka real-time updates but find the transaction search too slow. Alice is adding Elasticsearch on top of Atlas Postgres for full-text search. Expected improvement: 10x faster search.

The Apex analytics export feature is approved for Q2. It will sit on top of the Atlas Postgres read replica. Morgan owns the product spec, Dave owns the implementation. Exports will be CSV/JSON with filters on transaction type and date range.

Eve updated the product roadmap in Notion: Q1 done (Atlas, Phoenix, Horizon beta, Orion alpha), Q2 target (Prism, Apex, Horizon v2, Orion GA), Q3 stretch (Nexus API gateway, Vega analytics, international expansion). Shared in leadership Notion.

The Atlas bulk import feature is now designed. Alice is leading. It uses a Kafka topic for bulk processing — customers upload CSV, Phoenix processes each row through the Atlas transaction ledger. Bob designed the Kafka consumer pipeline.

Morgan: the Prism real-time analytics dashboard is now in beta. Kafka Streams processing, Postgres persistence, Horizon frontend built by Carol. Response times are under 500ms for all standard aggregate queries. On track for Q2 GA.

The Orion ML feature is going GA in Q2. Dave has achieved 96% prediction accuracy on the production PyTorch model. The GCP inference infrastructure is stable. Carol is building the Horizon UI components for the Orion predictions panel.

Product decision: Meridian will support the REST API alongside GraphQL for backward compatibility. Carol will document both in the developer portal. Alice confirmed the Atlas backend supports both via FastAPI. No performance tradeoff.

The v2 launch timeline is still TBD. Alice says Atlas scaling work will be complete by end of Q2. Dave says Orion will be production-ready. Carol says Horizon v2 design is 60% done with Skyler. Eve needs final dates by the end of April.

The Nexus API gateway is approved for Q3. Frank will build it on Nginx with JWT and OAuth. It will be the single entry point for all Meridian public APIs. Alice's Atlas, Dave's Orion, and Carol's Horizon APIs will all route through Nexus.

Customer success team: James and Wendy report that customers are most excited about the Horizon real-time dashboard and the Atlas ACID guarantees. The Kafka-powered notifications are a key differentiator vs competitors using MongoDB.

The international expansion roadmap question: do we localize Horizon in Q3 or Q4? Carol says the TypeScript component architecture makes i18n straightforward. Eve needs to confirm customer demand before committing engineering resources.

Ryan from partnerships: three new data integrations are signing up for the Atlas API. They need webhook support — Phoenix Kafka push notifications. Bob has the Kafka producer ready, Carol needs to build the Horizon webhook management UI.

The Vega analytics service is now prioritized for Q3. It will be a separate Postgres instance (not the Atlas primary) for long-running analytical queries. The decision was made by Alice to isolate OLAP load from the OLTP Atlas ledger.

Avery has completed the first draft of the Atlas API documentation. Covers: authentication (OAuth/JWT), REST and GraphQL endpoints, Postgres data types, transaction lifecycle, error codes. Carol is reviewing for technical accuracy.

The onboarding funnel analysis shows 30% drop-off at the Horizon integration step. Blake proposes a guided setup wizard. Carol says it's a 2-week build in TypeScript. Eve has approved — this goes into the next sprint.

The Orion prediction API is now available in Horizon for all beta users. Dave's FastAPI endpoint delivers predictions in under 60ms. Carol integrated it into the Horizon dashboard. Morgan is tracking prediction usage for the Prism analytics.

Should we invest in a native mobile app for Horizon? Open question discussed in product review. The React TypeScript web app works well on mobile via PWA. Blake will survey beta customers. TBD whether to invest in React Native this year.

The self-serve signup flow for Atlas is now live. Customers can sign up, connect a data source, and see their first Horizon dashboard within 10 minutes. Nathan reports the conversion rate is 22% higher than the old sales-assisted flow.

Eve's Q2 OKRs for product: 1) Atlas at 50k TPS, 2) Horizon v2 launch, 3) Prism GA, 4) Orion GA, 5) 10 new enterprise customers onboarded. Alice, Carol, Dave, and Morgan own the technical delivery. Nathan owns the customer metrics.

Feature flag system is being implemented in Horizon by Carol. TypeScript feature flags backed by Redis. This lets us roll out Orion ML features, Prism analytics, and Apex exports incrementally. Frank is provisioning the Redis feature flag store.

The data export format decision: Atlas will support Parquet and CSV for Apex exports. Dave proposed Parquet for compatibility with Orion ML training pipelines. Alice confirmed Postgres can generate Parquet via COPY command. Decided.

Zenith — the enterprise customer success portal — is now in the backlog. It would give enterprise customers visibility into their Atlas transaction health, Phoenix event stream status, and Orion prediction accuracy. TBD priority for Q3.
